Part One.

The four chapters in this part provide basic information on the tax system.

[The Income Tax](https://www.irs.gov/itin) They take you through the first steps of filling out a tax return. They also provide information about dependents, and discuss recordkeeping

Return requirements, IRS [e-file](https://www.identitytheft.gov/) (electronic filing), certain penalties, and the two methods used to pay tax during the year: withholding and estimated tax.

The Form 1040 and 1040-SR schedules that are discussed in these chapters are:

- Schedule 1, Additional Income and Adjustments to Income; and

- Schedule 3, Part II, Other Payments and Refundable Credits.

### Filing Information

[Table 1-3.](https://www.ssa.gov/forms/ss-5.pdf) Other Situations When You Must File a 2025 Return

You must file a return if any of the following apply for 2025.

| 1.  | You owe any special taxes, including any of the following (see the instructions for Schedule 2 (Form 1040)).               |
| --- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| a.  | Alternative minimum tax.                                                                                                   |
| b.  | Additional tax on a qualified plan, including an individual retirement arrangement (IRA), or other tax-favored account.    |
| c.  | Household employment taxes.                                                                                                |
| d.  | Social security and Medicare tax on tips you didn’t report to your employer or on wages you received from an employer who  |
| e.  | Uncollected social security and Medicare or RRTA tax on tips you reported to your employer or on group-term life insurance |
| f.  | Recapture taxes.                                                                                                           |

2. You (or your spouse, if filing jointly) received health savings account, Archer MSA, or Medicare Advantage MSA distributions.

3. You had net earnings from self-employment of at least $400.

4. You had wages of $108.28 or more from a church or qualified church-controlled organization that is exempt from employer social security and Medicare taxes.

5. Advance payments of the premium tax credit were made for you, your spouse, or a dependent who enrolled in coverage through the Marketplace. You or whoever enrolled you should have received Form(s) 1095-A showing the amount of the [advance payments.](https://www.irs.gov/chooseataxpro)

6. [You are required to include amounts in income under section 965 or you have a net tax liability under section 965 that you are](https://www.irs.gov/pub969) paying in installments under section 965(h) or deferred by making an election under section 965(i).

7. [You purchased a new or used clean vehicle from a registered dealer and reduced the amount you paid at the time of sale by](https://www.irs.gov/forms-pubs/about-publication-3) transferring the credit to the dealer. See Form 8936 and Schedule A (Form 8936).

The term “ qualifying relative ” is defined in chapter 3.

### Dependents

[Table 3-1.](https://www.ssa.gov/forms/ss-5.pdf) [Overview of the Rules for Claiming a Dependent](https://www.irs.gov/1040x)

Caution. This table is only an overview of the rules. For details, see the rest of this chapter.

- You can’t claim any dependents if you (or your spouse if filing jointly) could be claimed as a dependent by another taxpayer, unless that taxpayer files a return only to claim a refund of withheld income tax or estimated tax paid.

- You can’t claim a married person who files a joint return as a dependent unless that joint return is filed only to claim a refund of withheld income tax or estimated tax paid.

- You can’t claim a person as a dependent unless that person is a U.S. citizen, U.S. resident alien, U.S. national, or a resident of Canada or Mexico.

- You can’t claim a person as a dependent unless that person is your qualifying child or qualifying relative.

### Tests To Be a Qualifying Child Tests To Be a Qualifying Relative

1. The child must be your son, daughter, stepchild, foster child, 1. The person can’t be your qualifying child or the brother, sister, half brother, half sister, stepbrother, stepsister, qualifying child of any other taxpayer. or a descendant of any of them.

2. The person either (a) must be related to you in one of 2. [4.](https://www.irs.gov/secureaccess) [You must provide more than half of the person's total](https://www.irs.gov/pub551) 4. The child must not have provided more than half of the child’s [support for the year.](https://www.irs.gov/pub560) own support for the year.

5. [The child must not be filing a joint return for the year (unless](https://www.irs.gov/forms) that joint return is filed only to get a refund of income tax withheld or estimated tax paid).

If the child meets the rules to be a qualifying child of more than one person, generally only one person can actually treat the child as a qualifying child. ### Tax Withholding and Estimated Tax

Part Two.

### The five chapters in this part discuss many kinds of income and

Income and adjustments to income. They explain which income is and isn’t taxed and discuss some of the adjustments to income that you can make in figuring

Adjustments to your adjusted gross income.

- [•](https://www.irs.gov/notices) Schedule 2, Part II, Other Taxes; and

modified AGI may include income in addition to your compensation [(discussed ear-](https://www.irs.gov/ein) lier), such as interest, dividends, and income from IRA distributions.

### Individual Retirement Arrangements (IRAs)

[Worksheet 9-1.](https://www.ssa.gov/forms/ss-5.pdf) [Figuring Your Modified AGI](https://www.irs.gov/1040x) Keep for Your Records

Use this worksheet to figure your modified AGI for traditional IRA purposes.

1. Enter your AGI from Form 1040 or 1040-SR, line 11a, figured without taking into account the amount from Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 20 [. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .](https://www.irs.gov/account) 1.

2. Enter any student loan interest deduction from Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 21 . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [2.](https://www.taxpayeradvocate.irs.gov/)

3. Enter any foreign earned income and/or housing exclusion from Form 2555, line 45 . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 3.

[4.](https://www.irs.gov/eftps) Enter any foreign housing deduction from Form 2555, line 50 . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 4.

[5.](https://www.irs.gov/pub17) Enter any excludable savings bond interest from Form 8815, line 14 . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [5.](https://www.treasurydirect.gov/help-center/public-debt-faqs/#DebtFinance)

6. Enter any excluded employer-provided adoption benefits from Form 8839, line 30 [. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .](http://www.taxpayeradvocate.irs.gov/) 6.

7. Add lines 1 through 6. This is your [modified AGI](https://www.irs.gov/notices) for traditional IRA purposes . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 7.

### Can You Contribute to a Roth IRA?

Generally, you can contribute to a Roth IRA if [you have](https://www.irs.gov/orderforms) taxable compensation [(defined later)](https://www.irs.gov/pub54)

### Individual Retirement Arrangements (IRAs)

Worksheet 9-2. Modified AGI for Roth IRA Purposes Keep for Your Records

[Use this worksheet to figure your modified AGI for Roth IRA purposes.](https://www.irs.gov/wmar)

1. Enter your AGI from Form 1040 or 1040-SR, line 11a [. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .](https://www.ssa.gov/agency/contact) 1.

2. Enter any income resulting from the conversion of an IRA (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA (included on Form 1040 or 1040-SR, line 4b) and a rollover from a qualified retirement plan to a Roth IRA (included on Form 1040 or 1040-SR, line 5b) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [2.](https://www.treasurydirect.gov/savings-bonds/hh-bonds/)

[3.](https://www.irs.gov/eftps) [Subtract line 2 from line 1](https://www.irs.gov/pub17) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [3.](https://www.treasurydirect.gov/help-center/public-debt-faqs/#DebtFinance)

4. Enter any traditional IRA deduction from Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 20 . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [4.](https://www.treasurydirect.gov/help-center/public-debt-faqs/#DebtFinance)

[5.](https://www.irs.gov/paybymail) Enter any student loan interest deduction from Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 21 . . . . . . . . . 5.

6. Enter any foreign earned income and/or housing exclusion from Form 2555, line 45 . . . . . . 6.

Is the amount on line 10 more than the amount on line 11? If yes , then see the Note below. If no , then the amount on line 10 is your modified AGI for Roth IRA purposes.

Note: If the amount on line 10 is more than the amount on line 11 and you have other income or loss items, such as social security income or passive activity losses, that are subject to AGI-based phaseouts, you can refigure your AGI solely for the purpose of figuring your modified AGI for Roth IRA purposes. (If you receive social security benefits, use Worksheet 1 in Appendix B [of Pub. 590-A to refigure your AGI.) Then, go to line 3 above in this Worksheet 9-2 to refigure your modified AGI. If](https://www.irs.gov/pub502) you don't have other income or loss items subject to AGI-based phaseouts, your modified AGI for Roth IRA purposes is the amount on line 10.

If you are married filing a separate return and your spouse itemizes deductions, or if you are a dual-status alien, you can't take the standard deduction

### Table 10-1. Standard Deduction Chart for Most People*

IF your filing status is... [THEN your standard deduction is...](https://www.irs.gov/orderforms)

Single or Married filing separately $15,750

Married filing jointly or Qualifying surviving spouse 31,500

Head of household 23,625

* Don't use this chart if you were born before January 2, 1961, are blind, or if someone else can claim you (or your spouse, if filing jointly) as a dependent. Use Table 10-2 or [10-3 instead.](https://www.irs.gov/eftps)

## 2025 Tax Computation Worksheet—Line 16

## CAUTION

!

Note: [If you’re required to use this worksheet to figure the tax on an amount from another form or worksheet, such as the Qualified](https://www.identitytheft.gov/) Dividends and Capital Gain Tax Worksheet, the Schedule D Tax Worksheet, Schedule J, Form 8615, or the Foreign Earned Income Tax [Worksheet, enter the amount from that form or worksheet in column (a) of the row that applies to the amount you’re looking up. Enter the](https://www.irs.gov/depositlimit) result on the appropriate line of the form or worksheet that you’re completing.

## 2025 Tax Rate Schedules

The Tax Rate Schedules are shown so you can see the tax rate that applies to all levels of taxable income. Don’t use them to figure your tax. Instead, see chapter 13 .

### Where To File

[Mail your return to the address shown below that applies to you. If you want to use a private delivery service, see](https://www.irs.gov/modernpayments) Private delivery services in chapter 1 .

## TIP

[Envelopes without enough postage will be returned to you by the post office. Your envelope may need](https://www.identitytheft.gov/) additional postage if it contains more than five pages or is oversized (for example, it is over 1/4 inch thick). Also, include [your complete return address.](https://www.irs.gov/eftps)

| THEN send your return to the address    | OR send your return to the address |
